rationale.R1.2: Checking this artifact centrally requires testing whether the argument coherently moves from inter-model agreement to predictive skill and reliable forecasts despite internal counterexamples.
rationale.R1.3: The delegation centrally implicates whether the headline claims are supported by the reported regional results, especially given exclusions and sign disagreement.
rationale.RX.1: Uncertainty disclosure is active because the artifact discusses structural uncertainty and confidence in climate projections.
rationale.RX.2: Overclaim avoidance is active because the artifact makes strong claims about predictive skill, physical constraint, and adaptation use from limited agreement metrics.
rationale.RX.3: Scope adherence is active because the projection must identify checking responsibilities without performing the requested check or rewriting the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: Downstream-harm avoidance is active because overstated climate projection confidence could affect adaptation planning decisions.
rationale.RX.5: Provenance and traceability is active because any check responsibility depends on tracking which claims arise from the reported figures, exclusions, and definitions.
